solutions.solution: After examining the above figure, it is found that except (d), all figures can easily be obtained by clockwise and anti-clockwise movement of each other.
All figure except figure (d), can be rotated to form different figures.
All except, figure (c) contains odd number of arrows.
Except figure (e), all have three sides but in figure (e) there are four sides.
Except figure (e), all figures are made-up of three simple lines but in option (e) four lines are involved.
Except figure (a), all figures have equal number of figures both inside and outside of the main figure.
Except figure (a), all figures are divided into two equal parts.
Here each figure except (a) is divided such that each figure contains as many parts as equal to the number of sides in each figure.
